patient_narrative: I fractured my tibia and my doctor ordered physical therapy because I still have pain, weakness, and trouble walking safely. The insurer denied therapeutic exercises as not medically necessary.

denial_intake.denial_reason_text: Physical therapy therapeutic exercise services were denied as not medically necessary.

personal_evidence_task.patient_narrative: I still have pain and weakness after my tibia fracture and need therapy to walk safely.

symptoms: Persistent lower-leg pain after tibial fracture
Reduced balance and fear of falling

treatment_history.event: Closed tibial fracture treated by orthopedics
Physical therapy plan of care initiated for therapeutic exercise and gait recovery

prior_treatment_attempts.result: Insufficient improvement without supervised therapeutic exercise

personal_evidence.treating_physician_statement: Therapeutic exercise is medically necessary to restore strength, range of motion, balance, and safe ambulation after tibial fracture.

search_queries: CPT 97110 therapeutic exercise medical necessity tibial fracture rehabilitation
UnitedHealthcare outpatient physical therapy rehabilitation coverage criteria functional impairment
CMS outpatient physical therapy skilled therapy plan of care medical necessity
